title: trifolium phleoides subsp. gemellum
intro: Trifolium phleoides subsp. gemellum, also known as gemellate clover, is a rare and endemic subspecies of the Trifolium phleoides species, belonging to the Fabaceae family of plants. This plant is known for its potential medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of gemellate clover may include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health due to its high content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. Trifolium phleoides subsp. gemellum can be prepared for medicinal use through herbal infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
